name: Barranco de las Almunias - Collado Sillón - Mirador de las Planas — circular desde Cadrete
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cadrete offers diverse terrain characterized by a unique steppe landscape, featuring dry and often rocky trails. The region includes hills and crests, such as the Crestas de Cadrete, providing challenging enduro-style mountain biking. Numerous barrancos, or ravines, contribute to the rugged topography, creating natural descents and ascents. This environment presents technical challenges with varied sections, making for an engaging riding experience. Best mountain bike trails around Cadrete The most popular mountain bike trail is Barranco de las Almunias - Collado Sillón - Mirador de las Planas — circular desde Cadrete, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes scenic viewpoints and challenging barranco sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valdegurriana Locks – Barranco de la Muerte loop from Cuarte de Huerva, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route combines historical locks with rugged barranco terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Valdegurriana Locks – View of Balsa del Royo loop from Cuarte de Huerva, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail leading through historical Valdegurriana Locks and offering expansive views, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Cadrete is defined by its steppe landscape, challenging hills and crests, and numerous barrancos. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Cadrete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 280 reviews. More than 3900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cadrete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cadrete?
A: Cadrete offers a wide variety of mountain biking experiences, with over 80 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for mountain biking in Cadrete?
A: The trails around Cadrete offer a good mix of challenges. You'll find 17 easy routes, 51 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes, providing options for beginners to advanced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cadrete?
A: Cadrete is characterized by a unique steppe landscape, offering dry and often rocky trails. You'll encounter challenging hills and crests, as well as numerous barrancos (ravines) that provide natural descents and ascents. This varied terrain ensures an engaging and dynamic riding experience.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cadrete?
A: The dry, desert-like steppe landscape of Cadrete makes it ideal for mountain biking during most of the year. However, it's best to avoid periods immediately after heavy rain, as some barranco sections can become muddy and challenging. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cadrete?
A: Yes, there are several options suitable for families. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6585712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solar Labyrinth&lt;/a&gt; offers a unique and fun cycling experience that can be easily navigated. Additionally, sections along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3017867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Imperial Canal of Aragon&lt;/a&gt; provide flatter, scenic routes perfect for a relaxed family ride.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in Cadrete?
A: Many trails in Cadrete are suitable for riding with dogs, especially those in open, natural areas. However, always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry enough water for both of you, particularly in the dry steppe environment. Please respect local regulations and other trail users.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic viewpoints or interesting landmarks?
A: Absolutely! Many routes incorporate stunning views and historical sites. For expansive vistas, consider trails that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2080272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Verde Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore routes that take you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1355723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdegurriana Locks&lt;/a&gt; on the Imperial Canal of Aragon.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Cadrete?
A: The mountain biking routes in Cadrete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 280 reviews. Riders often praise the unique steppe landscape, the challenging barranco sections, and the rewarding views from various viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cadrete?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Cadrete are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984622393/barranco-de-las-almunias-collado-sillon-mirador-de-las-planas-circular-desde-cadrete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Almunias - Collado Sillón - Mirador de las Planas — circular desde Cadrete&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 36.7 km and includes scenic viewpoints and challenging barranco sections.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Cadrete?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the municipality of Cadrete, as well as near common starting points for trails. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32050697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdegurriana Locks – Barranco de la Muerte loop from Cuarte de Huerva&lt;/a&gt; often have parking options in nearby towns or designated areas.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that combine historical sites with natural beauty?
A: Definitely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdegurriana Locks – View of Balsa del Royo loop from Cuarte de Huerva&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 36.2 km trail leads through the historical Valdegurriana Locks and offers expansive views, blending cultural heritage with the region's natural landscapes.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike route for experienced riders looking for a challenge?
A: For experienced riders seeking a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38214120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla de Valmadrid – Pico Verde Viewpoint loop from Cuarte de Huerva&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. This 48.8 km route includes significant elevation changes and leads to the scenic Pico Verde Viewpoint, offering a rewarding ride.
tips: Beautiful natural arch produced by water erosion in the Barranco de Las Almunias.

The Collado del Sillón in María de Huerva is a very interesting hiking trail that offers spectacular views and an enriching experience. From the summit, you can enjoy panoramic views of Moncayo and the Pyrenees.
Nice site at the foot of a wind mill. From the Las Planas viewpoint, we will have views of Zaragoza and surrounding towns. And on clear days, views of Moncayo and the Pyrenees

The castle of Cadrete was a fortification of Islamic origin from the year 935, which after the expulsion of the Aragonese Moors in 1610 would pass into oblivion, remaining in ruins and gradually deteriorating until it was reformed in 2003.
Now it offers us beautiful views of the surroundings and an ideal place to practice sports.
